title: rootless CNI infra image not present
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

-> $ echo; podman run -dt --log-level=debug --restart=always --security-opt label=disable --pod=${_POD} --name someName docker.io/library/wordpress
...
...
DEBU[0000] Container 212c4910e502c32688ce3d6de3791a7d3d1b6c542c96a5d467da26f303d15d58 storage is already unmounted, skipping... 
ERRO[0000] error starting some container dependencies   
ERRO[0000] "rootless CNI infra image not present - please build image from https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra/ and tag as \"rootless-cni-infra\"" 
DEBU[0000] ExitCode msg: "error starting some containers: internal libpod error" 
Error: error starting some containers: internal libpod error

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've reset podman system, removed bits manually, rpm-reinstalled I believe all involved bits. I've also tried what that error message says on 'github' - which fails to build.
I'm on CentOS Stream with:
container-selinux-2.158.0-1.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.noarch
runc-1.0.0-70.rc92.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.x86_64
containers-common-1.2.2-6.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.x86_64
libcap-2.26-4.el8.x86_64
containernetworking-plugins-0.9.1-1.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.x86_64
podman-3.0.1-6.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.x86_64
podman-catatonit-3.0.1-6.module_el8.5.0+736+58cc1a5a.x86_64

comments.body: As the error message states you have to build the image yourself.
As I said in original report - I've also tried what that error message says on 'github' - which fails to build

Please provide the steps you used. This should work:
```
git clone https://github.com/containers/podman.git
cd podman
git checkout v3.0.1-rhel
cd contrib/rootless-cni-infra/
podman build -t rootless-cni-infra .
```
@lejeczek Were you able to build the image successfully?

Hi. Yes I can confirm following above instruction works.
But if I might ask - this is user manual intervention: how 'rootless-cni-infra' gets into the system initially, originally?
many thanks, L.

The upstream version does pull the image automatically from quay.io, however this does not work for environments without internet access. RedHat decided that the users should manually build/distribute the image to circumvent such limitation.

With podman v3.2 this image is no longer needed and rootless cni will work in all environments without extra setup.
